title: AFT survival model on laps-until-next-pit, then convert to next-lap risk
rationale: This target is naturally time-to-event. XGBoost’s AFT objective supports uncensored and right-censored labels, which fits rows whose next pit occurs later in the race versus rows censored at race end. That gives a different inductive bias than the current direct binary classifiers and the earlier hazard-style idea.
implementation_hint: Within each Year/Race/Driver sequence, derive `laps_until_next_pit` from the next future `PitStop==1`; if no future pit exists, mark as right-censored at remaining laps. Train `xgb.train` with `objective='survival:aft'` on the existing tabular features plus stint history. Convert predicted event time to a next-lap score by calibrating `P(T<=1)` or by isotonic-mapping the raw AFT prediction to `PitNextLap` using OOF folds.
expected_effect: Could add orthogonal signal on pit-window timing and improve blended AUC even if the standalone binary AUC is only similar to the baseline.
risk: AFT optimizes time likelihood, not AUC directly; the mapping from predicted survival time to next-lap probability can be noisy if calibration is weak.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
